channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: In Tacoma, a joyful treasure hunt for 'monkeyshines' has been going on for 2 decades
item.description: In Tacoma, Wash., glass artists hide beautiful glass spheres around the city each winter for residents to find. They're called "monkeyshines," because the first ones were stamped with a monkey stamp.
item.content:encoded: In Tacoma, Wash., glass artists hide beautiful glass spheres around the city each winter for residents to find. They're called "monkeyshines," because the first ones were stamped with a monkey stamp.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Gallup

item.title: Mundane, magic, maybe both — a new book explores 'The Writer's Room'
item.description: Why are we captivated by the spaces where authors write? Katie da Cunha Lewin set out to explore "The Hidden Worlds That Shape the Books We Love."
item.content:encoded: Why are we captivated by the spaces where authors write? Katie da Cunha Lewin set out to explore "The Hidden Worlds That Shape the Books We Love."
item.dc:creator: Andrew Limbong

item.title: The 2026 Oscar-nominated documentaries are sensitive and transformative
item.description: This year's Oscar-nominated documentaries include a tale of terminal cancer, dispatches from behind bars in Alabama, and stories from Iran and Russia.
item.content:encoded:
This year's Oscar-nominated documentaries include a tale of terminal cancer, dispatches from behind bars in Alabama, and stories from Iran and Russia.

(Image credit: Netflix)
item.dc:creator: Davi Merchan

item.title: Paul McCartney's decade of transformation: From Beatles breakup to John Lennon's murder
item.description: Man on the Run shows McCartney's effort to define himself outside The Beatles' shadow: "Paul making this documentary was a way of coming to terms with that whole period," says director Morgan Neville.
item.content:encoded: Man on the Run shows McCartney's effort to define himself outside The Beatles' shadow: "Paul making this documentary was a way of coming to terms with that whole period," says director Morgan Neville.
item.dc:creator: Ann Marie Baldonado

item.title: On the brink of death, a woman is saved by a stranger and his family
item.description: In 1982, Jean Muenchrath and her boyfriend went mountaineering in California's Sierra Nevada. Then, an unexpected storm veered them dangerously off course. Luckily, their unsung hero found them.
item.content:encoded: In 1982, Jean Muenchrath and her boyfriend went mountaineering in California's Sierra Nevada. Then, an unexpected storm veered them dangerously off course. Luckily, their unsung hero found them.
item.dc:creator: Autumn Barnes

item.title: Would you go to a play by yourself? Theaters are hoping so
item.description: As solo leisure booms globally, theater operator ATG Entertainment is launching "Solo Seats" events to destigmatize going to the theater alone.
item.content:encoded:
As solo leisure booms globally, theater operator ATG Entertainment is launching "Solo Seats" events to destigmatize going to the theater alone.

(Image credit: Roger Mastroianni)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: A glimpse of Iran, through the eyes of its artists and journalists
item.description: Understanding one of the world's oldest civilizations can't be achieved through a single film or book. But recent works of literature, journalism, music and film by Iranians are a powerful starting point.
item.content:encoded:
The Seed of the Sacred Fig, For The Sun After Long Nights, Cutting Through Rocks, It Was Just an Accident, Martyr!, and Kayhan Kalhor.'/>

Understanding one of the world's oldest civilizations can't be achieved through a single film or book. But recent works of literature, journalism, music and film by Iranians are a powerful starting point.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: This quiet epic is the top-grossing Japanese live action film of all time
item.description: The Oscar-nominated Kokuho tells a compelling story about friendship, the weight of history and the torturous road to becoming a star in Japan's Kabuki theater.
item.content:encoded:
The Oscar-nominated Kokuho tells a compelling story about friendship, the weight of history and the torturous road to becoming a star in Japan's Kabuki theater.

(Image credit: Shuichi Yoshida/ASP)
item.dc:creator: John Powers

item.title: For filmmaker Chloé Zhao, creative life was never linear
item.description: Director Chloé Zhao used meditation, somatic exercises and dance to inspire the cast and crew of this Oscar-nominated story about William Shakespeare's family.
item.content:encoded:
Director Chloé Zhao used meditation, somatic exercises and dance to inspire the cast and crew of this Oscar-nominated story about William Shakespeare's family.

(Image credit: Bethany Mollenkof for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Mandalit del Barco

item.title: 10 new books in March offer mental vacations
item.description: March is always a big one for books – this year is no different. We call out a handful of upcoming titles for readers to put on their radars — offering a good alternative to doomscrolling.
item.content:encoded: March is always a big one for books – this year is no different. We call out a handful of upcoming titles for readers to put on their radars — offering a good alternative to doomscrolling.
item.dc:creator: Colin Dwyer
